_meta.structure: 5-block: quarter, call_date, headline, guidance_changes, strategic_themes (3), qa_flashpoints (2)
_meta.notes: Backfilled 2026-05-24 by 5 parallel concall-summarizer subagents using WebSearch over transcripts/IR pages. Refreshed weekly going forward by Saturday agent.

agent_dispatch.batch_1: STMPA.PA, SNDK, DOCN, NOD.OL, MU, AMD, DELL, BESI.AS, STX, PRY.MI
agent_dispatch.batch_2: VICR, FLEX, COHR, WDC, TTMI, CIEN, SUBC.OL, ON, STRL, CAR
agent_dispatch.batch_3: TGS.OL, LITE, AJB.L, GEV, NBIX, MTSI, IGG.L, GLW, ASM.AS, SPM.MI
agent_dispatch.batch_4: AMKR, TWLO, VAR.OL, MNTN.L, AKRBP.OL, HPE, LSCC, BWLPG.OL, AKAM, NHY.OL
agent_dispatch.batch_5: BP.L, SCT.L, MTZ, FTNT, EQNR.OL, DPLM.L, ARW, HUM, SNX, TCAP.L

STMPA.PA.headline: Revenue beat at $3.1B with Q2 guide of $3.45B signaling cyclical bottom is behind ST, but Q1 EPS missed at $0.13 vs $0.17 consensus on restructuring drag and unused capacity charges.
guidance_changes: Q2 revenue guide: ~$3.1B Q1 actual -> $3.45B (+11.6% QoQ, +24.9% YoY)
Q2 gross margin guide: 33.8% Q1 actual -> 34.8% (incl. ~100bps unused capacity charges)
strategic_themes: NXP MEMS sensor business acquisition closed in Q1, adding ~$40M to quarterly revenue and broadening sensors portfolio
Embedded Processing (+31.3% YoY) and RF & Optical Communications (+33.9% YoY) led growth as analog/industrial inventory destock ends
Manufacturing footprint reshaping continues with restructuring charges weighing on near-term GM
qa_flashpoints: On Q2 GM bridge and unused capacity (Bernstein/Citi): mgmt quantified ~100bps unused capacity headwind staying in Q2 - implying utilization recovery is back-half-loaded
On automotive end-market trajectory (UBS): mgmt highlighted sequential improvement but stopped short of declaring a recovery in MCU/power - implying European OEM destock not fully complete

SNDK.headline: Sandisk delivered a NAND-supercycle blowout - Q3 revenue of $5.95B (+251% YoY, +97% sequentially) with 78.4% GM, and locked in a $42B multi-year revenue backlog underpinned by $11B of enforceable hyperscaler guarantees.
guidance_changes: Q4 FY26 revenue guide: $5.95B Q3 actual -> $7.75B-$8.25B (midpoint +35% QoQ)
Q4 FY26 non-GAAP diluted EPS guide: $23.41 Q3 actual -> $30.00-$33.00
strategic_themes: Datacenter revenue up 645% YoY to $1.47B as enterprise SSD with TLC ramps and QLC Stargate platform readies for hyperscaler AI inferencing
Five multi-year NBM agreements now cover one-third of FY27 bit supply with $11B in enforceable financial guarantees
BiCS8 218-layer node ramp coupled with deliberate bit-shipment discipline to preserve pricing into a supply-constrained 2027
qa_flashpoints: On sustainability of NAND ASP spike (Morgan Stanley): mgmt declined to call a near-term peak and pointed to contracted backlog stretching into 2027 - implying they see structural rather than cyclical pricing
On capex discipline vs demand pull (Wells Fargo): mgmt held the line on bit-supply growth despite triple-digit demand - implying willingness to leave revenue on the table to defend pricing

DOCN.headline: DigitalOcean's AI bet is paying off - Q1 revenue of $258M (+22% YoY) blew past consensus, AI ARR up 221% to $170M, and mgmt raised 2026 growth guide while flagging 50%+ growth for 2027 on the new AI-Native Cloud launch.
guidance_changes: FY2026 revenue growth guide: prior ~20% -> 25%-27% YoY
FY2027 revenue growth outlook: not previously framed -> 50%+ on AI-Native Cloud ramp
strategic_themes: AI-Native Cloud platform launched with 15+ new products across 5 integrated layers
Secured 60 MW of incremental data center capacity bringing total committed capacity to 135 MW through 2027
$1M+ ARR customer cohort grew 179% YoY to $183M in ARR, validating upmarket move beyond traditional SMB/developer base
qa_flashpoints: On GPU pricing durability (Jefferies/Goldman): mgmt said no evidence of price compression - implying confidence inference pricing holds firm even as hyperscaler GPU supply normalizes
On capacity utilization vs $888M raise (Citi): mgmt framed the equity raise as enabling rapid capacity secure-up - implying demand visibility is high enough that they're now supply-constrained

NOD.OL.headline: Nordic posted a clean beat with Q1 revenue of $192M (+24% YoY) and GM of 52.1%, validating the long-term >20% growth / >50% GM / 25% EBITDA model with new cellular IoT platforms broadening the TAM.
guidance_changes: Q2 2026 revenue guide: $192M Q1 actual -> $200M-$220M
Q2 2026 gross margin guide: 52.1% Q1 actual -> above 50% (held)
strategic_themes: Bluetooth Low Energy short-range wireless remains the cash engine and drove the bulk of Q1 revenue beat
nRF92 and nRF93 cellular IoT platforms ramping, expanding addressable market beyond BLE into NB-IoT/LTE-M
Operating leverage flowing through - adjusted EBITDA up to $23.9M (margin 11%, +44% YoY) with management reiterating march toward 25% target
qa_flashpoints: On sustainability of >20% growth into 2027 (DNB/Carnegie): mgmt explicitly reiterated the through-decade ambition - implying internal design-win pipeline beyond BLE is firming up
On Wi-Fi product roadmap traction (ABG SC): mgmt was measured about near-term Wi-Fi revenue contribution - implying Wi-Fi is a 2027+ story, not a 2026 driver

MU.headline: Micron is in a profit supercycle - quarterly revenue nearly tripled YoY with new records across DRAM, NAND, HBM, and every business unit.
guidance_changes: Q3 FY26 revenue guide: record $33.5B +/- $750M (new quarterly record)
Q3 FY26 gross margin guide: ~81%
Q3 FY26 non-GAAP EPS guide: record $19.15 +/- $0.40
FY26 capex guide: prior ~$20B -> above $25B
Quarterly dividend: raised 30%
strategic_themes: HBM3E 12-Hi at scale plus HBM4 36GB 12-Hi volume shipments for NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform - Micron sold out of HBM for several quarters
HBM4 16-Hi (48GB) sampled with HBM4E in development for 2027 ramp
Balance sheet strongest ever - $5B+ debt reduction over three quarters, strongest net cash position in company history
qa_flashpoints: On HBM share against Samsung re-entry (Morgan Stanley): mgmt emphasized customer qualification leadership and yield maturity advantage on HBM4 - implying they don't see Samsung as a near-term share threat into 2027
On NAND vs DRAM mix and CapEx allocation (Bernstein): mgmt raised CapEx to >$25B but pointed primarily to HBM and leading-edge DRAM nodes - implying NAND bit-supply discipline continues

AMD.headline: AMD beat across the board with Q1 revenue of $10.3B (+38% YoY) led by 57% Data Center growth, and Lisa Su laid out a path to >$20 EPS over the strategic horizon as MI350 ramps into hyperscaler pods.
guidance_changes: Q2 2026 revenue guide: $10.3B Q1 actual -> ~$11.2B (+46% YoY)
Q2 2026 non-GAAP gross margin guide: ~56%
Long-term EPS framework: >$20 EPS strategic-horizon target reaffirmed/elevated
strategic_themes: Instinct MI350 series ramping into hyperscaler pods alongside MI325 transition; AI accelerators now ~73% of Data Center revenue at $4.2B+ in Q1
Meta 6 GW multi-year deal anchoring MI450 (Helios) roadmap visibility into 2027
EPYC Turin server CPU share gains and Ryzen client momentum diversifying growth across every segment
qa_flashpoints: On China MI325 license status (Morgan Stanley): mgmt confirmed license applications submitted but explicitly excluded incremental China GPU revenue from guidance - implying they assume no approvals
On MI350 vs MI450 transition risk (Bernstein/Goldman): mgmt described the transition as overlapping rather than cliff-edged with both ramping at hyperscalers - implying no air-pocket between cycles

DELL.headline: Dell closed FY26 with record Q4 revenue of $33.4B (+39%) and EPS of $3.89 (+45%), entering FY27 with a $43B AI server backlog and guiding ~$50B in AI server revenue for the year (+100% YoY).
guidance_changes: Q1 FY27 revenue guide: $33.4B Q4 actual -> $34.7B-$35.7B (midpoint $35.2B, +51% YoY)
Q1 FY27 EPS guide: $3.89 Q4 actual -> $2.90 +/- $0.10
FY27 AI-optimized server revenue guide: ~$25B FY26 shipped -> ~$50B (+100% YoY)
FY27 total revenue guide: ~$140B midpoint (+23% YoY)
strategic_themes: AI-optimized server bookings of $64B+ in FY26 with $43B backlog entering FY27
Dell IP storage portfolio (PowerScale, PowerMax, PowerStore, ObjectScale) growing double-digit
PC refresh cycle for Windows 11 / AI PC plus commercial demand expected to inflect in CY2026
qa_flashpoints: On AI server gross margin dilution (Morgan Stanley/Bernstein): mgmt guided FY27 GM rate up YoY excluding AI mix - implying core ex-AI business margins are expanding

On backlog conversion velocity (Citi/Goldman): mgmt sidestepped giving a specific shipment cadence on the $43B backlog - implying supply (GPU allocation, liquid-cooling capacity) rather than demand is the binding constraint

BESI.AS.headline: BESI's hybrid bonding flywheel ignited - Q1 orders surged 105% YoY to 269.7M EUR (above prior 2024 peak) with the hybrid bonding customer base expanding to 20 across logic, memory, photonics and mobile.
guidance_changes: Q2 2026 revenue guide: 184.9M EUR Q1 actual -> +30% to +40% sequential (240M-259M EUR)
Q2 2026 gross margin guide: 63.5% Q1 actual -> 64%-66%
strategic_themes: Hybrid bonding unit orders more than doubled sequentially, with 2 evaluation tools shipped to a second memory customer for HBM applications
NVIDIA Rubin Ultra / Feynman GPU roadmap relies on 3D die-stacking with hybrid bonding via TSMC SoIC
High-end mobile and 2.5D AI photonics / data center applications drove the Q1 revenue beat
qa_flashpoints: On HBM hybrid bonding mainstream timeline (ASML/Degroof Petercam): mgmt pointed to mainstream HBM hybrid bonding volume production in 2027 - implying the second memory customer engagement is real but commercial revenue is still 4-6 quarters out
On capacity vs order ramp (Berenberg/Morgan Stanley): mgmt was confident on margin expansion to 64-66% in Q2 despite the order surge - implying disciplined capacity additions and pricing power

STX.headline: Seagate posted a blowout HAMR-driven Q3 with revenue of $3.11B (+44% YoY), non-GAAP EPS of $4.10, and ~$1B FCF - with Q4 guide near $3.45B / ~$5.00 EPS and two of the largest CSPs now qualified on Mozaic 4+ HAMR.
guidance_changes: Q4 FY26 revenue guide: $3.11B Q3 actual -> ~$3.45B (+11% QoQ)
Q4 FY26 non-GAAP EPS guide: $4.10 Q3 actual -> ~$5.00
Non-GAAP gross margin: 47.0% Q3 actual (record), Q4 guide implies further expansion
strategic_themes: Mozaic 4+ HAMR platform now qualified at two of the three largest CSPs
Nearline cloud/hyperscale exabytes up 6% sequentially in Q3 with the vast majority of HAMR supply allocated to cloud customers
Capacity discipline + tight supply backdrop + HDD/SSD TCO advantage for AI training storage tier
qa_flashpoints: On HAMR yield and qualification at third CSP (Wells Fargo/BofA): mgmt confirmed two CSPs qualified and described the third as 'on track' - implying the qualification risk that bears flagged through 2024-2025 is essentially closed
On supply tightness vs CY2027 capacity adds (Citi/Evercore): mgmt declined to call peak pricing - implying they expect supply to lag demand well into 2027

PRY.MI.headline: Prysmian beat with Q1 revenue of 5.2B EUR (+9.4% YoY, +5.0% organic) and adj. EBITDA of 601M EUR (+14%, margin 14.2% / +110bps), with mgmt signaling they will exceed the top end of FY26 guidance on Transmission and Digital Solutions strength.
guidance_changes: FY26 adjusted EBITDA guide: 2,625M-2,775M EUR (midpoint 2,700M) - confidence raised to upper end
2028 CMD adjusted EBITDA target: 3B+ EUR reaffirmed on track
strategic_themes: Transmission segment delivered 146M EUR EBITDA at 20.1% margin with two new lines online in Q1, a new cable-laying vessel joining in Q4 2026
Digital Solutions / data center pull from hyperscalers is the key margin-mix lever
Power Grids (10.6% margin) and Electrification (8.7% margin) remained pressured by metal premiums
qa_flashpoints: On hyperscaler long-term agreement economics (Mediobanca/Morgan Stanley): mgmt referenced 'accelerating' long-term agreements but stopped short of disclosing pricing structure - implying these are firm-volume, formula-priced contracts insulating Prysmian from copper volatility

On Electrification margin recovery (Kepler/Equita): mgmt attributed weakness to metal inflation and Specialties demand rather than competitive pressure - implying confidence the 8.7% margin is the trough

VICR.headline: Record Q1 with backlog up 70% QoQ to $300.6M validates the AI/HPC power thesis, but full-year guide stays conservative pending second ITC ruling in 2027.
guidance_changes: FY26 revenue framework: introduced ~$570M target (vs ~$480M FY25 base)
Q2 2026 revenue: implied ~$126M (vs Q1 actual $113M)
Fab One capacity ceiling: reaffirmed at ~$1.5B annual revenue with second fab planning underway
strategic_themes: Advanced Products ramping for hyperscaler 48V/800V vertical power delivery at HPC/AI customers
Licensing program positioning OEMs and hyperscalers as Vicor licensees, gated on second ITC exclusion order
Second-fab capacity expansion via acquired buildings to accelerate ramp beyond Fab One's $1.5B ceiling
qa_flashpoints: On licensing economics and timing (Needham): mgmt declined to model any new licensee revenue into FY26 guide - implying upside is back-half-loaded and ITC-dependent
On book-to-bill above 2.0 and capacity tightness (B. Riley): mgmt acknowledged demand is outrunning Fab One - signaling shipments rather than bookings will be the FY26 bottleneck

FLEX.headline: Flex announced a planned spin-off of its Cloud & Power Infrastructure segment (targeted 1Q calendar 2027), unmasking a 65-75% growth SpinCo and leaving RemainCo as a slower, healthcare/automation story.
guidance_changes: CPI SpinCo FY27 revenue growth: introduced at +65% to +75% YoY
CPI SpinCo FY28 revenue growth: introduced at >80% YoY
RemainCo Flex revenue growth: low-to-mid single-digit through the spin period
FY26 adjusted operating margin: 6.3% (up 70 bps YoY)
strategic_themes: Cloud & Power Infrastructure spin into standalone public company targeting hyperscaler data-center power and liquid cooling
RemainCo reorientation toward regulated healthcare, warehouse automation, and networking
Record Q4 adjusted gross margin of 9.9% driven by mix shift to higher-value power and cloud platforms
qa_flashpoints: On SpinCo scale and customer concentration (JPMorgan): mgmt defended diversification across hyperscaler end-markets but did not disclose specific customer % concentration - implying the SpinCo S-1 will be the first real look
On RemainCo's growth profile post-spin (Goldman): mgmt's 'low-to-mid single-digit' framing for the stub business landed as conservative - signaling mgmt is setting an easy bar to beat

COHR.headline: NVIDIA's $2B equity stake plus a multi-year supply agreement reframes Coherent as the optical partner of choice for scale-out and scale-up AI networks through end of decade.
guidance_changes: Q4 FY26 revenue: $1.91B-$2.05B (vs Q3 actual $1.81B)
Q4 FY26 non-GAAP gross margin: 39%-41% (vs Q3 at 39.6%)
Q4 FY26 non-GAAP operating expenses: $360M-$380M
strategic_themes: NVIDIA strategic partnership: $2B equity investment plus supply agreement through end of decade
Datacom & Communications segment hit $1.4B (60% YoY) led by 1.6T transceivers and data-center interconnect
Telecom inflection with revenue +60% YoY and +16% sequentially as DCI and 'scale-across' demand re-accelerate
qa_flashpoints: On CPO revenue ramp timing (Rosenblatt): mgmt anchored 'initial' scale-out CPO revenue to 2H 2026 with scale-up not until 2H 2027 - signaling the bigger NVIDIA-related step-up is a FY27 story
On industrial segment weakness ($444M, down from $529M): mgmt attributed it to portfolio pruning - implying the AI optical story is masking ongoing legacy drag

WDC.headline: HDD-pure-play WDC printed >50% gross margin and 45% YoY revenue growth, anchoring management's call for a 25%+ cloud exabyte CAGR driven by agentic AI workloads.
guidance_changes: Q4 FY26 revenue: $3.65B +/- $100M (vs Q3 actual $3.3B)
Q4 FY26 non-GAAP gross margin: 51%-52% (vs Q3 >50%)
Q4 FY26 non-GAAP EPS: $3.25 +/- $0.15 on ~385M shares
Long-term cloud exabyte growth: reaffirmed >25% CAGR
strategic_themes: Cloud capacity-oriented HDDs led by UltraSMR product family driving per-TB ASP +9% YoY through long-term agreements
Agentic AI and inferencing positioned as the next step-function demand driver for nearline storage
Pricing discipline via multi-year LTAs locking in volumes and limiting downside visibility risk
qa_flashpoints: On the sustainability of >50% gross margin (Morgan Stanley): mgmt pointed to LTA-driven pricing and UltraSMR mix rather than transient supply tightness - implying the new GM range is structural, not cyclical
On HAMR / next-gen technology transition (Wells Fargo): mgmt deflected on a hard HAMR ramp date and emphasized ePMR/UltraSMR runway - signaling capex discipline and a deliberate lag behind Seagate

TTMI.headline: Record Q1 with ~80% of sales tied to AI and defense; capex bumped to $310M to accelerate Penang/Syracuse/Wisconsin ramps for AI data-center and aerospace customers.
guidance_changes: Q2 2026 revenue: $930M-$970M (vs Q1 actual $846M)
FY26 capex: $250M midpoint -> $310M
Q1 non-GAAP gross margin: 22.3% (+150 bps YoY)
strategic_themes: Data Center & Networking now 36% of sales on AI accelerator/switch PCB content
Aerospace & Defense at 40% of mix backed by multi-year radar and missile programs with U.S. primes
Penang, Syracuse and Wisconsin facility ramps anchoring multi-year AI/defense capacity build
qa_flashpoints: On the $60M capex step-up (Stifel): mgmt framed it as pull-forward of equipment for AI customer commitments - implying demand visibility is firm enough to take the FCF hit
On Penang ramp drag on segment margin (B. Riley): mgmt reiterated A&D segment OM at 15.6% - signaling investors should look through Penang noise for a clean FY27 margin reset

CIEN.headline: Record quarter with revenue +33% YoY and Direct Cloud Provider business +76% YoY, with most new orders booked for 2027 delivery extending visibility into the AI optical buildout.
guidance_changes: FY26 revenue: raised to $5.9B-$6.3B (midpoint ~28% YoY growth)
FY26 adjusted gross margin: 43.5%-44.5%
FY26 adjusted operating expenses: $1.52B-$1.53B
Q2 FY26 revenue: $1.5B +/- $50M
strategic_themes: WaveLogic 6 Extreme 1.6T coherent adds 18 customers in the quarter to reach 90
Direct Cloud Providers at 42% of revenue, +76% YoY, signaling a structural mix shift away from telco carrier dependence
Strong RLS system and pluggable optical demand in India and MOFN routes tied to long-haul AI traffic
qa_flashpoints: On 2027 order visibility (Cowen): mgmt confirmed nearly all new orders are slated for 2027 delivery - implying the AI optical capex cycle has multi-year duration

On margin trajectory vs mix (Rosenblatt): mgmt held GM guide at 43.5-44.5% despite higher cloud/pluggable mix - signaling concession on price/mix is the cost of locking in multi-year hyperscaler share

SUBC.OL.headline: Q1 EBITDA +63% YoY drove a full-year guidance raise, with $13.5B backlog including $5B for 2027 cementing the multi-year offshore cycle.
guidance_changes: FY26 adjusted EBITDA margin: ~22% -> ~23%
Backlog: $13.5B firm orders, with $5.5B remaining 2026, $5.0B 2027 (+17%), $3.0B 2028+
strategic_themes: Subsea segment delivered 24% EBITDA margin on tight offshore EPCI capacity for deepwater operators
Renewables/Seaway7 +15% YoY to $282M with Seaway Ventus and Seaway Aimery operating through North Sea winter
Offshore wind tailwinds firming: UK AR7 cleared 8.2 GW, Germany moving to CfD, Taiwan 3.6 GW for 2026
qa_flashpoints: On the durability of subsea pricing (DNB Markets): mgmt pointed to tight vessel supply through 2027 and 24% margin as evidence the cycle is mid-not-late - implying further EBITDA upside

On renewables margin path (Pareto): mgmt acknowledged 12% renewables EBITDA still trails subsea but framed UK/Germany/Taiwan award cadence as the catalyst - signaling Seaway7 standalone returns remain a 2027 story

ON.headline: ON beat Q1 with $1.51B revenue and $0.64 EPS, guided Q2 +7% sequentially, and reiterated AI data-center revenue will double YoY in 2026 led by 55% SiC share in new China EV models.
guidance_changes: Q2 2026 revenue: ~+7% QoQ at midpoint (excluding $30M-$40M of planned non-core exits)
FY26 AI data center revenue: reaffirmed to double YoY
Q1 non-GAAP EPS: $0.64 (above guide midpoint)
strategic_themes: AI data-center high-voltage power conversion ramping with 800V system-level integration for hyperscaler racks
Silicon carbide leadership at 55% share of new EV models at the 2026 Beijing Auto Show, anchoring China auto growth
New IGBT-based traction inverter design win with a North American OEM transitioning to direct semiconductor sourcing for 900V EV architectures
qa_flashpoints: On the AI data-center 'double' claim (Citi): mgmt held the line but declined to size the absolute revenue base - implying the doubling is off a still-small denominator
On China EV pricing pressure (Mizuho): mgmt pointed to 55% SiC share and content gains rather than ASPs - signaling ON is competing on socket-count and design-wins

STRL.headline: Revenue +92% and EPS +120% YoY, with mission-critical (data center, semi, manufacturing) now >90% of E-Infrastructure backlog, and management raised full-year guidance.
guidance_changes: FY26 guidance: raised (revenue, EBITDA and EPS) following Q1 16.78% EPS surprise
Backlog: $3.80B with 2.1x book-to-burn; combined backlog incl. unsigned awards $5.15B at 3.5x
Combined project visibility: $6.5B (vs prior disclosure)
strategic_themes: Data center and semiconductor site work now >90% of E-Infrastructure backlog (vs 84% at YE25), led by a new $500M semiconductor campus award
CEC acquisition contributed $156M of Q1 E-Infrastructure revenue
Margin expansion: gross profit +105% YoY to $194.3M outpacing the 92% revenue growth
qa_flashpoints: On the durability of mission-critical mix (DA Davidson): mgmt highlighted multi-year hyperscaler and semi customer commitments behind the $5.15B combined backlog - implying the mix shift is not a single-cycle spike
On capacity to convert backlog (Stifel): mgmt deferred specifics on labor/equipment constraints - signaling execution risk is the chief governor on outyear conversion

CAR.headline: Americas revenue grew for the first time in 10 quarters (+2.9% YoY) on disciplined fleet cuts and +2.8% RPD, prompting Avis to raise FY26 EBITDA guide to $850M-$1B.
guidance_changes: FY26 adjusted EBITDA: raised to $850M-$1B (with Q1 plan beat by ~$50M)
Americas RPD: +2.8% YoY in Q1, exiting March near +4%
Net corporate leverage: targeting 7.6x -> below 6x by year-end
strategic_themes: Americas fleet ~20% younger YoY enabling pricing discipline and resetting depreciation per unit
Avis First premium product rollout positioned as a 2H 2026 financial contributor
Waymo partnership expansion in Dallas and additional cities positioned as a 2027 monetization story
qa_flashpoints: On the durability of pricing exiting Q1 at +4% (Barclays): mgmt expected stabilization in May/June after the Easter shift - signaling the +4% exit rate is not the run-rate to model

On the path from 7.6x to <6x leverage (Morgan Stanley): mgmt leaned on EBITDA growth plus 'dedicated' debt repayment without committing to asset sales - implying the balance sheet fix is contingent on the EBITDA range holding

TGS.OL.headline: Exploration cycle is back: backlog hit $779M (highest since 2019) on 91% vessel utilization and record order inflow.
guidance_changes: 2026 multi-client investments: maintained at $500M-$575M
2026 gross operating cost: held at ~$950M
Quarterly dividend: held at $0.155/share
strategic_themes: Multi-client library monetization rebounding on energy-security demand from IOCs and NOCs across North Sea and offshore Brazil
Marine acquisition fleet running at 91% utilization with internal production of $137M supporting late-sale upside
Imaging segment leveraging proprietary OBN datasets as exploration shifts toward reserve replacement
qa_flashpoints: On backlog sustainability (Pareto): mgmt pointed to $392M Q1 inflow but declined to commit to repeat run-rate - implying Q1 was front-loaded
On capex discipline (Clarksons): mgmt held 2026 investment range despite stronger demand - signaling preference to flow upside to dividend/buyback

LITE.headline: NVIDIA $2B direct investment plus record $808M revenue (+90% YoY) reframes Lumentum as a strategic AI optics supplier with structural supply tightness through CY26.
guidance_changes: Q4 FY26 revenue: prior implied ~$830M -> $960M-$1.01B
Q4 FY26 non-GAAP EPS: -> $2.85-$3.05
Q4 FY26 non-GAAP operating margin: -> 35%-36%
strategic_themes: NVIDIA strategic agreement and $2B equity injection funding US-based EML and transceiver capacity expansion
Cloud transceiver revenue grew >40% sequentially with 800G/1.6T ramping at hyperscaler customers
EML laser chip shipments doubled YoY with management guiding >50% unit growth Dec25-Dec26
qa_flashpoints: On capacity vs demand (Rosenblatt): mgmt said EMLs, pump lasers and transceivers all remain capacity-constrained into CY27 - implying pricing power is structural
On NVIDIA exclusivity (Needham): mgmt declined to characterize the agreement as exclusive but emphasized 'preferred supplier' framing - signaling capacity allocation favors NVDA

AJB.L.headline: AJ Bell raised FY26 guidance on record 79k net new platform customers and 4.2bn GBP net inflows, but cash-interest reliance (~47% of platform revenue) remains the bear case.
guidance_changes: FY26 revenue margin: prior implied ~32bps -> above H1 actual of 33.4bps
FY26 PBT margin: prior guide -> above 40%
Interim dividend: +11% YoY; new 15M GBP buyback approved
strategic_themes: Platform AUA grew to 108.7bn GBP with 723k customers; D2C 'Dodl' and advised platform both contributing to record net inflows
Brand and marketing reinvestment compressed PBT margin to 43.2% - explicit trade-off to defend customer growth lead vs Hargreaves Lansdown
UK policy noise on ISAs and pensions drove >1bn GBP of excess pension withdrawals
qa_flashpoints: On cash-interest sensitivity (Deutsche Numis): mgmt acknowledged ~47% of platform revenue is interest-linked and revenue margin will moderate as rates and overseas dealing normalize - implying FY27 consensus may need to rebase down
On pricing pressure (Peel Hunt): mgmt committed to further price cuts to defend share - signaling willingness to absorb margin compression rather than cede the D2C land grab

GEV.headline: Electrification orders surged 71% YoY to $18.3B and FY guidance raised, but US onshore Wind remains a $250-350M tariff headwind with no visible inflection.
guidance_changes: FY26 revenue: prior $36-37B -> $44.5B-$45.5B (raised)
FY26 free cash flow: prior $2.0-2.5B -> $6.5B-$7.5B (raised)
Backlog $200B target: pulled forward from 2028 -> 2027
strategic_themes: Power segment gas turbine backlog at record on hyperscaler data center demand - slots now sold through 2028
Electrification (grid + HVDC) growing 71% YoY with named wins in US transmission and EU offshore interconnect
Onshore Wind in retrenchment: 25% revenue decline and $382M EBITDA loss as US permitting delays and tariffs push shipments to 2H
qa_flashpoints: On tariff exposure (Morgan Stanley): mgmt quantified $250-350M 2026 hit but declined to name supplier substitution path - implying Wind margin recovery is back-half dependent
On gas turbine pricing (JPMorgan): mgmt said equipment margins are 'considerably better' on the incremental backlog but refused to give absolute number - signaling pricing is well above mid-cycle

NBIX.headline: First-ever $800M+ revenue quarter (+44% YoY) with CRENESSITY launch running ahead of plan and now annualizing above $600M.
guidance_changes: FY26 INGREZZA net sales: reaffirmed at $2.7B-$2.8B
CRENESSITY: now annualizing >$600M
Soleno / VYKAT XR acquisition: on track to close Q2 2026
strategic_themes: INGREZZA delivered $657M (+20% YoY) on record new-patient adds and double-digit volume
CRENESSITY launch in CAH outpacing internal model on strong persistence, broad payer coverage and rapid endocrinologist uptake
Pipeline catalysts queued: osavampator (MDD), NBI-1117568 / direclidine (schizophrenia) and NBIP-2118 (obesity) all with 2027 readouts
qa_flashpoints: On INGREZZA guidance conservatism (Goldman): mgmt declined to raise despite Q1 beat - implying they are reserving capacity for a mid-year raise
On Soleno integration (Cantor): mgmt deflected on accretion timing but emphasized commercial overlap with rare-disease infrastructure - signaling VYKAT XR will be folded into existing endo salesforce

MTSI.headline: Data Center inflection drives Q3 guide to $335M (Street $300M) and full-year DC growth raised to 35-40% on 1.6T optical ramp.
guidance_changes: Q3 FY26 revenue: Street ~$300M -> guided ~$335M midpoint
FY26 Data Center revenue growth: prior implied 25-30% -> 35-40%
Q2 actual: revenue $289M (vs $285M consensus), adj EPS $1.09 (vs $1.07)
strategic_themes: 1.6T optical design wins transitioning to production at hyperscalers - single biggest growth lever
Defense and aerospace order book strengthening with named radar and EW program wins
High-speed analog and coherent light products opening pull from non-hyperscaler cloud and telco infrastructure
qa_flashpoints: On capacity and lead times (Susquehanna): mgmt said DC backlog is 'very healthy' but declined to quantify book-to-bill - implying tightness is real but they want to avoid setting a ceiling
On gross margin trajectory (Northland): mgmt held GM guidance flat despite mix shift to DC - signaling DC margins are below corporate average

IGG.L.headline: IG raised FY26 organic revenue guide to +10-15% (from high-single-digit) on record 331M GBP quarter and launched a strategic review including possible domicile/listing change.
guidance_changes: FY26 organic revenue growth: high-single-digit -> 10-15% on 1.10bn GBP 2025 base
Medium-term organic revenue growth: -> at least 10% p.a. beyond FY26
Medium-term EBITDA margin: sustained mid-40% range
strategic_themes: Active customer base grew 12% YoY to 309,200; first trades up 63% YoY driven by UK and APAC
tastytrade US options franchise and crypto product expansion driving outsized trading revenue (+25% YoY)
Board strategic review covering acquisitions, domicile change and listing venue - explicit signal of dissatisfaction with London valuation
qa_flashpoints: On strategic review scope (Peel Hunt): mgmt confirmed US listing is on the table - implying serious consideration of leaving LSE if multiple gap to US peers persists
On net interest income decline (Numis): mgmt acknowledged 22% YoY drop and greater customer pass-through - signaling rate-cycle headwind is now structural

GLW.headline: Two new multi-billion-dollar hyperscaler optical agreements (similar to the $6B Meta deal) confirm AI infrastructure is now the dominant Corning growth story.
guidance_changes: Q2 26 sales: guided +14% YoY
Springboard incremental annualized sales target: +$3B by 2026; +$11B by 2028 (raised)
Springboard plan extended through 2030 with further upgrade at May 6 investor day
strategic_themes: Optical Communications +36% YoY to $1.8B; three signed long-term hyperscaler agreements (Meta + two new) with risk-sharing capacity commitments
Solar segment ramping US polysilicon-to-module value chain with Hemlock and named utility-scale customer wins
Display margin discipline maintained via price/yen mechanism while volume mix shifts to gen-AI-driven Optical and Solar
qa_flashpoints: On capacity for hyperscalers (BofA): mgmt said they have signed deals 'beyond what we publicly disclosed' but won't name counterparts - implying a third or fourth megadeal is already in hand
On gross margin trajectory (Wells Fargo): mgmt held GM expansion to ~220bps despite mix tailwind - signaling startup costs on new fiber capacity will weigh near-term

ASM.AS.headline: Record 33.1% operating margin and Q2 guide to 980M EUR reaffirm GAA-driven ALD leadership; first 1.4nm pilot-line contribution expected H2 2026.
guidance_changes: Q2 2026 revenue: -> 980M EUR +/-5% (sequential acceleration from 863M EUR)
FY26: high-end of prior range increasingly likely (mgmt commentary, not formal raise)
First 1.4nm pilot-line revenue: now expected in H2 2026
strategic_themes: Gate-All-Around ALD ramp at TSMC and Samsung leading edge driving share gains
Memory rebound: DRAM customers (Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron) returning with HBM-related ALD demand
China revenue normalizing from 2024 peak as mature-node spend rolls off; Korea and Taiwan now the dominant geographic mix
qa_flashpoints: On GAA layer-count tailwind (Citi): mgmt said incremental layers in 2026 are modest vs prior year - implying the GAA volume kicker is more 2027 story
On High-NA EUV and 1.4nm timing (Morgan Stanley): mgmt confirmed first pilot revenue in H2 26 but declined to size 2027 ramp - signaling visibility beyond 12 months is still customer-dependent

SPM.MI.headline: EBITDA +24% YoY to 434M EUR with backlog at 29.6B EUR; Subsea7 merger on track for H2 2026 close, creating 43B EUR-backlog Saipem7.
guidance_changes: FY26 revenue: low single-digit growth (reaffirmed)
FY26 EBITDA and EBIT: double-digit growth (reaffirmed)
Subsea7 merger close: H2 2026 timeline maintained
strategic_themes: Offshore E&C deepwater pipeline strong across Guyana (ExxonMobil), Brazil pre-salt (Petrobras) and Namibia (TotalEnergies / Galp)
Onshore E&C delivering on Qatar NFE and Saudi Jafurah scope with margin discipline restored
Subsea7 merger to create vertically integrated SURF leader; 300M EUR+ synergy target
qa_flashpoints: On merger regulatory risk (Equita): mgmt downplayed CMA and CADE concerns and said no material divestitures expected - implying confidence the deal closes without structural remedies

On vessel utilization and offshore wind (Mediobanca): mgmt sidestepped specific utilization figures and emphasized oil & gas pivot - signaling offshore wind backlog impairment risk is still being managed

AMKR.headline: Record Q1 with 27% YoY revenue growth as advanced packaging for AI tripled in 2026 outlook and iOS/HBM ramps drove a strong Q2 guide.
guidance_changes: Q2 2026 revenue: implied prior ~$1.55B -> $1.75-$1.85B (about +7% sequential)
Q2 2026 gross margin: 14.2% Q1 actual -> 14.5-15.5% guide
FY26 advanced packaging revenue: prior baseline -> 'expected to triple' year-over-year
strategic_themes: Advanced packaging for AI compute (HBM-related and new HDFO data center CPU program ramping into 2H26)
iOS/Apple ecosystem driving stronger-than-seasonal communications growth (+42% YoY in Q1)
Automotive and industrial recovery alongside Arizona facility build-out for US-based advanced packaging
qa_flashpoints: On 2H26 GM trajectory: mgmt guided 'mid-to-high teens' on data center ramps - implying confidence in compute mix structurally lifting margins
On AI compute customer concentration: mgmt declined to name programs but flagged 'new compute customers' beyond the lead Apple/HBM business - signaling AMKR is broadening AI ASIC/CoWoS-like exposure

TWLO.headline: Twilio's fastest organic growth since 2022 (+16%) on voice AI inflection drove a sharp raise to FY26 guide and stock-based comp falling below 10% of revenue for the first time.
guidance_changes: FY26 revenue growth: 11.5-12.5% -> 14-15%
FY26 non-GAAP operating income: $1.04-$1.06B -> $1.08-$1.10B
FY26 SBC as % of revenue: above 10% prior -> below 10% (first time since IPO)
strategic_themes: Voice AI as the new entry point to the Twilio platform - sixth consecutive quarter of voice acceleration, with Voice channel revenue +20% YoY
ConversationRelay and OpenAI/Anthropic agent integrations positioned as the 'AI orchestration layer' for contact centers
Cross-sell of Segment + Communications bundle expanding into enterprise wins as messaging carrier fee headwinds offset by AI premium pricing
qa_flashpoints: On voice AI durability (Morgan Stanley): mgmt framed AI use cases as 'unprecedented demand' but declined to break out AI revenue contribution - implying they want to avoid bull/bear AI-mix scrutiny
On gross margin compression from carrier fees: mgmt acknowledged GM pressure but pointed to voice AI as structurally higher margin - implying the GM trough is near

VAR.OL.headline: Record Q1 production of 406 kboepd (+50% YoY) drove EBITDA up 49% to $2.16B, though net profit fell 17.5% on higher D&A from the 2025 project ramp portfolio.
guidance_changes: FY26 production guidance reaffirmed: 390-410 kboepd
FY26 development CapEx unchanged: $2.5-$2.7B; exploration $250-$300M
Q2 2026 dividend: $300M (matching Q1, 17 consecutive quarters of stable/growing payout)
strategic_themes: Balder X / Jotun FPSO and Johan Castberg ramp delivering record Barents Sea production (88 kboepd, Castberg +13% QoQ)
Two new project sanctions in 2026 (Goliat Gas Export and King) targeting ~80 mmbbls net reserves
Three commercial exploration discoveries in Q1 reinforcing Norwegian Continental Shelf inventory
qa_flashpoints: On underlifting reversals and Brent-premium fields (Castberg, Grane, Balder): mgmt confirmed structural realized-price uplift - implying consensus revenue models are still under-weighting positive crude-quality differentials
On maintenance flexibility and 2H26 production cadence: mgmt held the 390-410 kboepd band rather than upgrading despite the 406 Q1 print - implying conservatism around planned summer turnarounds

MNTN.L.headline: Vehicle does not issue quarterly earnings - Baillie Gifford-managed late-stage private growth trust reports semi-annual NAV with monthly factsheet updates; shares trade at ~4% premium to NAV after a 120% 12-month rally.

AKRBP.OL.headline: Production of 398 kboepd at the high end of guidance, sector-leading $7.7/boe unit cost, and Symra brought on stream nine months early - reinforcing the 2027 Yggdrasil/Valhall PWP-Fenris growth story.
guidance_changes: FY26 production reaffirmed: 370-400 kboepd
FY26 production cost reaffirmed: ~$8/boe (Q1 ran below at $7.7)
FY26 CapEx reaffirmed: $6.2-$6.7B
Quarterly dividend held at $0.6615/share
strategic_themes: Symra first oil 9 months ahead of plan + Skarv Satellites accelerated to Q3 2026 - showcasing execution edge on tieback projects
Yggdrasil and Valhall PWP-Fenris megaprojects on track for 2027 first oil
97% production efficiency and lowest-quartile unit cost positioning Aker BP as the highest-FCF NCS operator at $60+ Brent
qa_flashpoints: On Yggdrasil CapEx creep and 2027 startup risk: mgmt reiterated 'on schedule and within budget' but flagged supply-chain tightness - implying any slippage would be a 6-month event

On dividend trajectory post-2027 production step-up: mgmt held the $0.6615 quarterly payout flat rather than signaling progression - implying capital allocation discipline ahead of the 2027 FCF inflection

HPE.headline: First post-Juniper-close quarter delivered networking revenue +151% YoY and a record $0.65 EPS, but stock dipped on supply-tightness commentary and tariff uncertainty.
guidance_changes: FY26 revenue growth: reaffirmed 17-22%
FY26 Networking segment growth: raised to 68-73%
FY26 non-GAAP EPS: raised to $2.30-$2.50
FY26 free cash flow: raised to at least $2.0B
Networks-for-AI cumulative order target: raised to $1.7-$1.9B (AI backlog now >$5B)
strategic_themes: Juniper integration Phase 1 complete - QFX data center switches and PTX/MX routing already driving cross-sell wins
AI systems backlog crossed $5B with sovereign and Tier-2 hyperscaler wins balancing the Cloud & AI segment's -2.7% reported decline
Self-driving networks (Mist AI + Aruba) positioned as the enterprise AI campus standard
qa_flashpoints: On tariff exposure post-Supreme Court decision (Morgan Stanley): Neri said HPE is 'monitoring closely' but declined to quantify - implying internal models still see meaningful FY26 GM risk
On component supply tightness and rising costs: Myers said HPE is taking 'decisive actions to protect profitability' - implying mid-year price increases are likely

LSCC.headline: Revenue +42% YoY with Compute & Comms +86% as data center FPGA attach rates rise; $1.65B AMI acquisition reframes Lattice as a server-platform secure management player.
guidance_changes: Q2 2026 revenue: ~$146M Q1 prior run-rate -> $185M midpoint (+50% YoY)
Q2 2026 non-GAAP EPS: $0.29 prior -> $0.44 (+80% YoY)
Gross margin trajectory: maintained at ~69.5-70% with AMI deemed immediately accretive
strategic_themes: $1.65B AMI (American Megatrends) acquisition - doubles TAM into server platform firmware/BMC
Avant FPGA platform ramping with rising attach rates per AI server rack
Compute & Communications now 62% of revenue - the AI data center mix shift is structurally complete
qa_flashpoints: On AMI organic growth and synergies (Susquehanna): mgmt guided AMI to 'high-teens' growth but explicitly disclaimed any synergy reliance - implying the deal model is conservative and upside is unspoken
On FPGA-per-server attach rates in AI racks (Deutsche Bank): mgmt said attach rates are 'rising' with new rack architectures - implying content-per-server is the unappreciated lever

BWLPG.OL.headline: Q4 NPAT of $123M and $0.57/share dividend (100% Shipping payout, 12.5% annualized yield), with Q1 2026 already 94% fixed at ~$54,000/day - a sharp recovery from late-2025 VLGC weakness.
guidance_changes: Q1 2026 TCE: ~$54,000/day fixed at 94% coverage (vs Q4 2025 actual $50,300/day)
FY26 fixed-rate time charter coverage: 36% at $43,700/day + 4% FFA hedges at $47,900/day
FY26 all-in cash break-even: $23,400/day (one of the lowest in the listed VLGC peer set)
FY26 drydock schedule: 13 vessels (193 off-hire days in Q1 alone)
strategic_themes: Product Services arm (LPG trading) increasingly insulating quarterly NPAT volatility from spot freight cycles
US Gulf-to-Asia arbitrage and Panama Canal transit dynamics keeping ton-mile demand structurally elevated
100% Shipping NPAT payout policy reaffirmed - a clear capital-return signal at sub-1.0x leverage
qa_flashpoints: On 2H26 rate trajectory given orderbook deliveries: mgmt pointed to LPG export growth from US Gulf + Middle East offsetting newbuild supply - implying 2026 rate environment will be tighter than VLGC orderbook math suggests
On dividend sustainability if Q1 spot weakens: mgmt reiterated 100% Shipping NPAT payout as policy - implying willingness to flex dividend down quarter-to-quarter rather than smooth

AKAM.headline: Cloud Infrastructure Services +40% YoY plus a record $1.8B / 7-year frontier AI customer commitment validates Akamai's pivot from CDN to AI inference cloud - stock surged 21%.
guidance_changes: FY26 revenue: guided $4.445-$4.55B (consensus $4.48B)
FY26 adjusted EPS: $6.40-$7.15 (consensus $6.86)
FY26 Cloud Infrastructure Services growth: raised to at least 50% (from prior ~40%)
FY27 outlook: management framed double-digit total revenue growth as the new bar
strategic_themes: $1.8B / 7-year contract with a leading frontier AI model provider - anchors the distributed inference / 'edge AI' positioning
Security portfolio (Guardicore, Bot Manager, API Security) growing +11% - offsetting legacy delivery declines
GPU and CPU capacity build-out at the edge funded by recent megadeals
qa_flashpoints: On the AI megadeal's revenue ramp shape: mgmt said ramp begins 'late 2026' with multi-year build - implying 2026 numbers don't yet reflect the deal
On CapEx required to fulfill the AI commitment: mgmt acknowledged increased GPU/CPU capex but pointed to long contract duration - implying ROIC will look weak in 2026 before scaling

NHY.OL.headline: Adjusted EBITDA of NOK 8.7B on LME aluminum surging from $2,995 to $3,400+/t, but Bauxite & Alumina EBITDA collapsed to NOK 747M on weak alumina prices - a tale of two upstream segments.
guidance_changes: Extrusions Q2 2026: higher sales volumes QoQ with stable margins, continued strong US recycling margins
B&A Q2 2026: production volumes expected to decline seasonally on fewer operating days + maintenance
Aluminum Metal Q2 2026: raw material/energy/FX cost headwinds called out vs Q1
strategic_themes: Recycling business delivered strongest results since 2023 - US scrap spreads remain the most attractive structural growth lever
Hull Zero test facility (Sunndal) commissioned as a marker toward net-zero aluminum production by 2050
Brazil B&A (Alunorte/Paragominas) margin compression on PAX in the $297-$315/mt band
qa_flashpoints: On alumina price floor and Alunorte profitability: mgmt described Chinese alumina oversupply and Middle East smelter curtailment as drivers - implying B&A weakness is structural through 2026
On the LME aluminum rally durability after Q1 spike to $3,400+: mgmt avoided forecasting metal prices directly - implying internal models still assume mean-reversion

BP.L.headline: BP delivered $3.2B underlying RC profit and accelerated its balance-sheet repair pivot, hiking the structural cost-out target by $1B as Auchincloss leans further into oil & gas over low-carbon spend.
guidance_changes: Structural cost reduction target by 2027: $5.5-6.5B -> $6.5-7.5B (raised $1B)
Corporate hybrid bond financing by end-2027: ~$13.3B -> ~$9B (reduction of ~$4.3B)
FY26 upstream production: prior 'broadly flat' -> reported production lower on Middle East disruption, underlying broadly flat vs 2025
strategic_themes: Portfolio high-grading via Gelsenkirchen refinery divestiture in Germany as part of $20B disposal program
Capital discipline pivot back to oil & gas (Gulf of America, Iraq, Trinidad LNG) and away from low-carbon capex
Balance sheet repair prioritized over distributions - net debt reduction target reiterated despite $6B working capital build
qa_flashpoints: On buyback pace (Bernstein): mgmt held the $0.75-1.0B/quarter run-rate but emphasized 'balance sheet first' more than a dozen times - implying buyback step-up is firmly back-end loaded into 2H26
On Rosneft/Russia stake (Barclays): mgmt declined to give an updated exit framework - implying no near-term resolution and continued accounting overhang

SCT.L.headline: Softcat smashed H1 with 33% gross invoiced income growth and upgraded full-year op profit guide from low single-digit to high single-digit growth, driving a 7%+ pop.
guidance_changes: FY26 underlying operating profit growth: low single-digit -> high single-digit (upgrade)
Interim dividend: 8.9p -> 9.9p (+11.2%)
Completed 45M GBP share buyback in February (incremental to prior capital return plan)
strategic_themes: Hyperscaler/software momentum (Microsoft, AWS) driving 33.3% gross invoiced income growth to 2,008.6M GBP
Cash conversion strength at 147.6% funding accelerated UK mid-market customer wins and the 45M GBP buyback
Continued investment in technical specialists (security, networking, cloud) to support cross-sell
qa_flashpoints: On sustainability of the H1 growth rate (Berenberg): mgmt flagged tougher H2 comps and FX tailwinds normalising - implying the high-single-digit FY guide is deliberately conservative
On AI-related demand pull-forward (Peel Hunt): mgmt described demand as 'broad-based' rather than AI-concentrated - implying the cycle has real legs beyond one workload

MTZ.headline: MasTec blew past Q1 with EPS of $1.39 (vs $0.99 consensus) on 34% revenue growth and a record $20.3B backlog, prompting a full-year guide raise.
guidance_changes: FY26 revenue guidance: ~$16.7B -> $17.5B
FY26 adjusted EBITDA: prior ~$1.35B -> $1.5B (8.6% margin)
Backlog: prior record -> $20.3B (up 28% YoY)
strategic_themes: Power Delivery and Clean Energy & Infrastructure segments capturing utility-scale build-out tied to data center load growth across Texas and Southeast
Pipeline Infrastructure inflection - large interstate gas projects (MVP, Permian-to-Gulf) re-accelerating
Communications segment stabilization with named customers AT&T and T-Mobile fiber/wireless densification spend ramping
qa_flashpoints: On data center power demand visibility (Goldman Sachs): CEO Jose Mas cited multi-year customer commitments and said booked pipeline 'extends well into 2027' - implying mgmt has visibility to defend an even higher 2027 EBITDA bar
On margin sustainability (Stifel): CFO DiMarco attributed the 73% EBITDA jump to operating leverage rather than mix - implying the 8.6% margin is a floor, not a peak

FTNT.headline: Fortinet posted a clean beat with revenue +20%, billings +31%, and a record 35.8% non-GAAP op margin - but reiterated (didn't raise) FY26 guidance, leaving room for upside.
guidance_changes: Q2 2026 revenue guide: new at ~$1.82B (implies ~17% YoY)
Q2 2026 non-GAAP EPS guide: new at ~$0.71
FY26 full-year guidance: reiterated (not raised despite Q1 beat)
strategic_themes: Unified SASE momentum - product revenue +41% driven by FortiSASE adoption among large enterprise customers
AI-driven security operations (FortiAI) billings +23% with 20+ AI-enabled SKUs now on the Security Fabric platform
OT/critical infrastructure security expansion in EMEA and APAC as the third growth pillar
qa_flashpoints: On why FY26 guide wasn't raised despite the Q1 beat (Morgan Stanley): CFO Keith Jensen cited macro and FX prudence - implying mgmt is sandbagging and the full-year number is highly de-risked
On firewall refresh cycle (Goldman Sachs): mgmt said the refresh is 'mid-innings' and extending into 2027 - implying the bear case of a refresh peak in 2026 is wrong

EQNR.OL.headline: Equinor delivered record Q1 production of 2.3 mmboe/d (+9% YoY) and $9.77B adjusted operating income, but the stock dipped on Norwegian tax timing impact even as guidance and the $1.5B buyback were reaffirmed.
guidance_changes: FY26 production growth: ~3% (reiterated)
FY26 organic CapEx: $13B (reiterated)
FY26 share buyback program: up to $1.5B (reiterated; second tranche of up to $375M launched)
strategic_themes: Norwegian Continental Shelf (NCS) high-grading - Johan Sverdrup and Johan Castberg driving record Q1 output
International ramp via US shale (Bakken) and Brazil (Bacalhau) supporting the ~3% production growth path
Renewables capex discipline - Empire Wind 1 (US) progressing but mgmt explicitly capping low-carbon spend within the $13B envelope
qa_flashpoints: On the tax timing drag (Citi): CFO Torgrim Reitan framed it as a one-off Norway timing impact, not a structural rate change - implying the premarket dip overreacted
On capital returns vs peers (RBC): mgmt held the $0.39/quarter cash dividend flat - implying Equinor is prioritizing balance sheet flex for potential downside in oil

DPLM.L.headline: Diploma posted a standout H1 with 17% revenue growth, 33% adjusted operating profit growth, and a second FY26 guidance upgrade driven by Controls' 26% organic surge.
guidance_changes: FY26 organic revenue growth guide: ~10% -> 12%
FY26 acquisition contribution: prior ~3-4% -> ~6%
FY26 adjusted operating profit growth: prior ~20% -> 30%+
Interim dividend: +5% YoY increase
strategic_themes: Controls division surge (+26% organic) driven by aerospace, defence, data centres and energy end-markets - a US defence interconnect bolt-on adds further capacity
Disciplined M&A engine - 15 deals for ~310M GBP over the LTM while keeping leverage at 0.8x
Margin expansion to 24.5% (+300 bps) showing scale benefits in Controls offsetting tougher Seals (+2%) and Life Sciences (+4%) markets
qa_flashpoints: On the sustainability of Controls' 26% organic growth (Numis): mgmt indicated defence and data-center order books extend well into FY27 - implying the upgrade cycle isn't a one-quarter phenomenon
On Seals & Life Sciences weakness (Peel Hunt): mgmt blamed end-market cyclicality, not share loss - implying these segments are a coiled spring once macro stabilizes

ARW.headline: Arrow Electronics blew the doors off with EPS of $5.22 vs $2.82 consensus and revenue +39% YoY to $9.5B, signaling a sharper-than-expected components cycle inflection.
guidance_changes: Q1 EPS: consensus ~$2.82 -> reported $5.22 (85% beat)
Q1 revenue: consensus ~$8.3B -> reported $9.5B (+14%)
Q1 operating margin: expanded 160 bps YoY to 4.2%
Q2 outlook: Supply Chain Services profit expected to normalize after Q1 customer-driven surge
strategic_themes: Global Components recovery - Americas and EMEA distribution demand inflecting across industrial, transportation, and aerospace/defence
Enterprise Computing Solutions (ECS) tailwind - hyperscaler/AI server pull-through plus 4 extra shipping days delivered several hundred million in incremental billings
Interim leadership stability under CEO Bill Austen executing on cost actions while permanent CEO search continues
qa_flashpoints: On the sustainability of components margins (Citi): mgmt said Q1 Supply Chain Services profit was 'customer-driven' and Q2 will normalize - implying the 4.2% op margin is not the new run-rate
On AI/hyperscaler exposure within ECS (Barclays): mgmt acknowledged AI server demand but cautioned on lumpy timing - implying the ECS beat is real but quarterly volatility will stay elevated

HUM.headline: Humana delivered Q1 adjusted EPS of $10.31 at the high end of guide with Insurance benefit ratio of 89.4% favorable to the 'just under 90%' target, affirming FY26 guidance.
guidance_changes: FY26 adjusted EPS guidance: affirmed (Q1 came in at high end of 110-115% of FY26 seasonal expectation)
FY26 Insurance segment benefit ratio: affirmed at 92.75% +/- 25 bps
Q1 Insurance benefit ratio: 89.4% (favorable vs 'just under 90%' guide)
strategic_themes: Medicare Advantage margin rebuild - STARS ratings remediation and bid discipline drive the 92.75% benefit ratio path
State-based Medicaid expansion - ~50,000 member adds from new Michigan, Illinois, and South Carolina contracts
CenterWell primary care growth and operating leverage as the core long-term margin driver beyond MA cycle
qa_flashpoints: On whether Q1 outperformance allows a guide raise (Wells Fargo): mgmt explicitly chose to affirm not raise, citing utilization uncertainty in 2H - implying mgmt sees prudence value in the post-2024 reset

On 2027 MA bid posture (Cantor): mgmt deflected on specifics but emphasized 'margin recovery is multi-year' - implying investors should not expect MA target margins back to historical levels before 2027/28

SNX.headline: TD SYNNEX crushed Q1 with EPS of $4.73 vs $3.32 consensus on 24% non-GAAP gross billings growth, led by a 95% surge in Hive driven by AI/cloud data center demand.
guidance_changes: Q2 FY26 non-GAAP gross billings guide: ~$25.1B +/- $500M (~16% YoY at midpoint)
Q2 FY26 non-GAAP diluted EPS guide: ~$4.00 +/- $0.25
Q1 EPS: consensus $3.32 -> reported $4.73 (42% beat)
strategic_themes: Hive (hyperscale infrastructure) segment +95% YoY in gross billings - cloud & AI data center build-out is now the dominant growth driver
Core distribution strength across infrastructure, security, and software with named vendor depth (Cisco, Microsoft, Dell, HPE)
Profitable share gains in the Americas SMB channel offsetting more measured EMEA recovery
qa_flashpoints: On Hive margin profile and sustainability (Citi): CFO indicated Hive is dilutive to overall margin but accretive to gross profit dollars and ROIC - implying the optical margin compression narrative is misleading
On why the stock fell despite the beat (Barclays): mgmt acknowledged the Q2 EPS guide of $4.00 implies sequential moderation - implying investors viewed the print as 'as good as it gets' near-term

TCAP.L.headline: TP ICAP posted its strongest-ever year with record $3.15B revenue, launched a 80M GBP buyback, and lifted the dividend 4% as volatility tailwinds and the pending Vantage Capital Markets deal underpin 2026.
guidance_changes: Total FY25 dividend: 16.2p -> 16.8p (+4%)
New share buyback: 80M GBP (~$107m) announced - 6th program; total returned since 2023 now ~600M GBP
FY26 outlook: ~9-10M GBP FX headwind to adjusted EBIT at current spot vs FY25
strategic_themes: Liquidnet rebuild - multi-asset agency brokerage +10%, equities stable, APAC momentum, with Neptune Networks acquisition extending Fixed Income electronic platform reach
Parameta Solutions (+5% revenue) deepening buy-side penetration; potential minority IPO still under board review but mgmt flagged IPO market conditions as 'challenging'
Pending Vantage Capital Markets acquisition (Jan 2026) to strengthen equity derivatives and fixed income presence across Asia-Pacific
qa_flashpoints: On Parameta IPO timing (Numis): CEO Nicolas Breteau described IPO market as 'challenging' and gave no fresh timeline - implying the value-unlock catalyst many holders are underwriting slips into 2027 at the earliest

On capital return capacity post-Vantage and post-Neptune (Peel Hunt): mgmt indicated the 80M GBP buyback can continue at this cadence - implying M&A is not crowding out capital return, but also no step-change higher buyback is coming
